mission.title: Install and Configure Opentelemetry Operator (Opentelemetry) on Kubernetes
mission.description: The OpenTelemetry Operator is a Kubernetes Operator that manages the OpenTelemetry Collector and facilitates auto-instrumentation of workloads using OpenTelemetry libraries. Installing this operator allows you to easily collect and export telemetry data from your applications.

steps.title: Add the OpenTelemetry Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the OpenTelemetry Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration. This will allow you to install the OpenTelemetry Operator using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the OpenTelemetry Operator
steps.description:
Now, install the OpenTelemetry Operator using Helm. Make sure to specify the namespace where you want to deploy the operator.
```bash
helm install opentelemetry-operator open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator --namespace observability --create-namespace --version v0.145.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenTelemetry Operator is running successfully by listing the pods in the observability namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability
```

steps.title: Deploy an OpenTelemetry Collector instance
steps.description:
Create a simple OpenTelemetry Collector instance by applying the following YAML configuration. This will set up a collector that receives telemetry data and exports it to the console.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: simplest
spec:
  config:
    receivers:
      otlp:
        protocols:
          grpc:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
          http:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
    processors:
      memory_limiter:
        check_interval: 1s
        limit_percentage: 75
        spike_limit_percentage: 15
    exporters:
      debug: {}
    service:
      pipelines:
        traces:
          receivers: [otlp]
          processors: [memory_limiter]
          exporters: [debug]
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the OpenTelemetry Collector instance
steps.description:
Ensure that the OpenTelemetry Collector instance is running by checking its pod status.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the OpenTelemetry Operator and the OpenTelemetry Collector pods running in the 'observability' namespace. You should see the 'simplest' collector instance ready to receive telemetry data.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install opentelemetry-operator open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator --namespace observability --create-namespace --version v0.145.0

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: simplest
spec:
  config:
    receivers:
      otlp:
        protocols:
          grpc:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
          http:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
    processors:
      memory_limiter:
        check_interval: 1s
        limit_percentage: 75
        spike_limit_percentage: 15
    exporters:
      debug: {}
    service:
      pipelines:
        traces:
          receivers: [otlp]
          processors: [memory_limiter]
          exporters: [debug]
EOF

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy the OpenTelemetry Operator.
